verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचक्रीडासक्तेषु गोपेषु तद्गावो दूरचारिणी: ।स्वैरं चरन्त्यो विविशुस्तृणलोभेन गह्वरम् ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācakrīḍāsakteṣu gopeṣutad-gāvo dūra-cāriṇīḥsvairaṁ carantyo viviśustṛṇa-lobhena gahvaram
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: While the cowherd boys were completely absorbed in playing, their cows wandered far away. They hungered for more grass, and with no one to watch them they entered a dense forest.

verses.sanskrit: अजा गावो महिष्यश्च निर्विशन्त्यो वनाद् वनम् ।ईषीकाटवीं निर्विविशु: क्रन्दन्त्यो दावतर्षिता: ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: ajā gāvo mahiṣyaś canirviśantyo vanād vanamīṣīkāṭavīṁ nirviviśuḥkrandantyo dāva-tarṣitāḥ
verses.text: Passing from one part of the great forest to another, the goats, cows and buffalo eventually entered an area overgrown with sharp canes. The heat of a nearby forest fire made them thirsty, and they cried out in distress.

verses.sanskrit: तेऽपश्यन्त: पशून् गोपा: कृष्णरामादयस्तदा ।जातानुतापा न विदुर्विचिन्वन्तो गवां गतिम् ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: te ’paśyantaḥ paśūn gopāḥkṛṣṇa-rāmādayas tadājātānutāpā na vidurvicinvanto gavāṁ gatim
verses.text: Not seeing the cows before them, Kṛṣṇa, Rāma and Their cowherd friends suddenly felt repentant for having neglected them. The boys searched all around, but could not discover where they had gone.

verses.transliteration: tṛṇais tat-khura-dac-chinnairgoṣ-padair aṅkitair gavāmmārgam anvagaman sarvenaṣṭājīvyā vicetasaḥ
verses.text: Then the boys began tracing out the cows’ path by noting their hoofprints and the blades of grass the cows had broken with their hooves and teeth. All the cowherd boys were in great anxiety because they had lost their source of livelihood.

verses.sanskrit: मुञ्जाटव्यां भ्रष्टमार्गं क्रन्दमानं स्वगोधनम् ।सम्प्राप्य तृषिता: श्रान्तास्ततस्ते सन्न्यवर्तयन् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: muñjāṭavyāṁ bhraṣṭa-mārgaṁkrandamānaṁ sva-godhanamsamprāpya tṛṣitāḥ śrāntāstatas te sannyavartayan
verses.text: Within the Muñjā forest the cowherd boys finally found their valuable cows, who had lost their way and were crying. Then the boys, thirsty and tired, herded the cows onto the path back home.

verses.sanskrit: ता आहूता भगवता मेघगम्भीरया गिरा ।स्वनाम्नां निनदं श्रुत्वा प्रतिनेदु: प्रहर्षिता: ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tā āhūtā bhagavatāmegha-gambhīrayā girāsva-nāmnāṁ ninadaṁ śrutvāpratineduḥ praharṣitāḥ
verses.text: The Supreme Personality of Godhead called out to the animals in a voice that resounded like a rumbling cloud. Hearing the sound of their own names, the cows were overjoyed and called out to the Lord in reply.

verses.sanskrit: तत: समन्ताद्देवधूमकेतु-र्यद‍ृच्छयाभूत् क्षयकृद् वनौकसाम् ।समीरित: सारथिनोल्बणोल्मुकै-र्विलेलिहान: स्थिरजङ्गमान् महान् ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tataḥ samantād dava-dhūmaketuryadṛcchayābhūt kṣaya-kṛd vanaukasāmsamīritaḥ sārathinolbaṇolmukairvilelihānaḥ sthira-jaṅgamān mahān
verses.text: Suddenly a great forest fire appeared on all sides, threatening to destroy all the forest creatures. Like a chariot driver, the wind swept the fire onward, and terrible sparks shot in all directions. Indeed, the great fire extended its tongues of flame toward all moving and nonmoving creatures.

verses.sanskrit: तमापतन्तं परितो दवाग्निंगोपाश्च गाव: प्रसमीक्ष्य भीता: ।ऊचुश्च कृष्णं सबलं प्रपन्नायथा हरिं मृत्युभयार्दिता जना: ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tam āpatantaṁ parito davāgniṁgopāś ca gāvaḥ prasamīkṣya bhītāḥūcuś ca kṛṣṇaṁ sa-balaṁ prapannāyathā hariṁ mṛtyu-bhayārditā janāḥ
verses.text: As the cows and cowherd boys stared at the forest fire attacking them on all sides, they became fearful. The boys then approached Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma for shelter, just as those who are disturbed by fear of death approach the Supreme Personality of Godhead. The boys addressed Them as follows.

verses.sanskrit: कृष्ण कृष्ण महावीर हे रामामोघविक्रम ।दावाग्निना दह्यमानान् प्रपन्नांस्त्रातुमर्हथ: ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa mahā-vīrahe rāmāmogha vikramadāvāgninā dahyamānānprapannāṁs trātum arhathaḥ
verses.text: [The cowherd boys said:] O Kṛṣṇa! Kṛṣṇa! Most powerful one! O Rāma! You whose prowess never fails! Please save Your devotees, who are about to be burned by this forest fire and have come to take shelter of You!

verses.sanskrit: नूनं त्वद्ब‍ान्धवा: कृष्ण न चार्हन्त्यवसादितुम् ।वयं हि सर्वधर्मज्ञ त्वन्नाथास्त्वत्परायणा: ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: nūnaṁ tvad-bāndhavāḥ kṛṣṇana cārhanty avasāditumvayaṁ hi sarva-dharma-jñatvan-nāthās tvat-parāyaṇāḥ
verses.text: Kṛṣṇa! Certainly Your own friends shouldn’t be destroyed. O knower of the nature of all things, we have accepted You as our Lord, and we are souls surrendered unto You!

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचवचो निशम्य कृपणं बन्धूनां भगवान् हरि: ।निमीलयत मा भैष्ट लोचनानीत्यभाषत ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācavaco niśamya kṛpaṇaṁbandhūnāṁ bhagavān hariḥnimīlayata mā bhaiṣṭalocanānīty abhāṣata
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Hearing these pitiful words from His friends, the Supreme Lord Kṛṣṇa told them, “Just close your eyes and do not be afraid.”

verses.sanskrit: तथेति मीलिताक्षेषु भगवानग्निमुल्बणम् ।पीत्वा मुखेन तान्कृच्छ्राद् योगाधीशो व्यमोचयत् ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatheti mīlitākṣeṣubhagavān agnim ulbaṇampītvā mukhena tān kṛcchrādyogādhīśo vyamocayat
verses.text: “All right,” the boys replied, and immediately closed their eyes. Then the Supreme Lord, the master of all mystic power, opened His mouth and swallowed the terrible fire, saving His friends from danger.

verses.sanskrit: ततश्च तेऽक्षीण्युन्मील्य पुनर्भाण्डीरमापिता: ।निशम्य विस्मिता आसन्नात्मानं गाश्च मोचिता: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tataś ca te ’kṣīṇy unmīlyapunar bhāṇḍīram āpitāḥniśamya vismitā āsannātmānaṁ gāś ca mocitāḥ
verses.text: The cowherd boys opened their eyes and were amazed to find not only that they and the cows had been saved from the terrible fire but that they had all been brought back to the Bhāṇḍīra tree.

verses.sanskrit: कृष्णस्य योगवीर्यं तद् योगमायानुभावितम् ।दावाग्नेरात्मन: क्षेमं वीक्ष्य ते मेनिरेऽमरम् ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: kṛṣṇasya yoga-vīryaṁ tadyoga-māyānubhāvitamdāvāgner ātmanaḥ kṣemaṁvīkṣya te menire ’maram
verses.text: When the cowherd boys saw that they had been saved from the forest fire by the Lord’s mystic power, which is manifested by His internal potency, they began to think that Kṛṣṇa must be a demigod.

verses.sanskrit: गा: सन्निवर्त्य सायाह्ने सहरामो जनार्दन: ।वेणुं विरणयन् गोष्ठमगाद् गोपैरभिष्टुत: ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: gāḥ sannivartya sāyāhnesaha-rāmo janārdanaḥveṇuṁ viraṇayan goṣṭhamagād gopair abhiṣṭutaḥ
verses.text: It was now late in the afternoon, and Lord Kṛṣṇa, accompanied by Balarāma, turned the cows back toward home. Playing His flute in a special way, Kṛṣṇa returned to the cowherd village in the company of His cowherd friends, who chanted His glories.

verses.sanskrit: गोपीनां परमानन्द आसीद् गोविन्ददर्शने ।क्षणं युगशतमिव यासां येन विनाभवत् ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: gopīnāṁ paramānandaāsīd govinda-darśanekṣaṇaṁ yuga-śatam ivayāsāṁ yena vinābhavat
verses.text: The young gopīs took the greatest pleasure in seeing Govinda come home, since for them even a moment without His association seemed like a hundred ages.
